cand_lca_sae.notes: Backup candidate; not piloted; pivot trigger (OMP reduces absorption >= 30%) not met — OMP shows 0% reduction, the opposite of what would activate LCA-SAE. LCA-SAE would only make sense if encoder improvements reduce absorption; empirical result rules this out for first-letter task.

cand_successive_refinement.notes: F1 completed: 67% of absorbed features recovered in wider 32k SAE. Use as supplementary finding. Confirms that 1/3 of absorption is not simply a dictionary capacity issue.

cand_recoverability.notes: Trigger condition (late absorption > 15% AND amortization gap is mixed 20-50% OMP reduction) not met. OMP shows 0% reduction, ruling out amortization gap entirely. Skip in favor of main contributions.

H_ENC.evidence: encoder_norm AUROC=0.757 at GPT-2 L6 (CI: [0.655, 0.837]); DeLong z=3.04, p=0.0012 vs. EDA; generalizes to TopK (AUROC=0.837)

H1_ARS_detection.evidence: ARS product form fails (ARS_v2 AUROC=0.586 < encoder_norm 0.757; DeLong z=-2.45, p=0.993). Root cause: A_cooccur bounded at 0.33; 9/18 absorbed features have O_jaccard=0. O_jaccard alone achieves AUROC=0.721, AUPRC=0.075 as complementary signal. Reframe detection contribution around encoder_norm + O_jaccard.

H2_amortization_gap.evidence: OMP absorption rate = 0.978, feedforward = 0.978. Reduction ratio = 0.000. Pre-committed interpretation: sparsity landscape is dominant driver. Tang et al. (2512.05534) hypothesis supported; O'Neill et al. (2411.13117) amortization gap is NOT the dominant cause of absorption.

H3_cross_hierarchy.evidence: Entity-type AR = 0.000, negative control AR = 0.050. H3 required entity > control by 10pp; actual direction reversed. However, k-sparse proxy absorption metric likely does not correctly measure entity-type absorption. Methodology fix required before concluding H3 is conceptually falsified.

key_findings: encoder_norm AUROC=0.757 replicates iter_002 finding exactly (4 decimal places), confirming robustness across iterations
OMP encoding at matched L0 produces 0% reduction in absorption rate — sparsity landscape dominance is the decisive conclusion; encoder improvements are insufficient
ARS ecological product form fails due to A_cooccur mathematical ceiling and O_jaccard coverage gap; encoder_norm + O_jaccard together provide stronger, simpler signal
encoder_norm generalizes across architectures: Standard L1 (0.757) and TopK 32k (0.837), with AUROC difference 0.080 < 0.10 threshold
67% of absorbed features in 24k SAE have geometric analogs in 32k SAE (cosine sim > 0.80), but 33% do not — partial dictionary capacity effect
Entity-type probe achieves 100% accuracy in D1, but absorption proxy measurement yields 0% AR — methodology fix needed for cross-hierarchy experiment
paper_narrative: Three-contribution paper where 2 are confirmed positive and 1 is a clean, decisive negative: (1) encoder_norm + O_jaccard as complementary training-free absorption detectors with theoretical ecological grounding; (2) decisive negative result: OMP encoding does not reduce absorption, confirming sparsity landscape dominance and redirecting the field from encoder to training-time solutions; (3) cross-hierarchy null finding suggesting first-letter absorption does not trivially generalize to entity-type semantic hierarchies (requiring further investigation of WHY).
reasons: H_ENC confirmed with AUROC=0.757, replicating prior iteration finding with exact Chanin IG labels (n_pos=18, DeLong p=0.0012)
H2 falsification (0% OMP absorption reduction) is a decisive publishable result that directly adjudicates Tang vs. O'Neill debate in favor of sparsity landscape
ARS product form failure is a fixable methodology issue — encoder_norm survives as the core detection signal; O_jaccard provides orthogonal AUPRC=0.075 complementary signal
encoder_norm generalizes to TopK architecture (AUROC=0.837) confirming cross-architecture validity
Novelty scores 8-9 across contributions; amortization gap controlled experiment is uniquely novel (novelty_report.json score=9 for C2)
Weighted score 3.90 > 3.5 threshold for ADVANCE; no candidate has a falsification criterion that is both unambiguous and simultaneous across all three contributions
next_actions: Run full B2 with corrected ARS: test encoder_norm + O_jaccard as ensemble, generate DeLong CI for O_jaccard AUROC=0.721
Run full C2 amortization gap analysis: 1000-token dataset, all 26 letters, Wilcoxon signed-rank test, stratify by encoder_norm quartile
Fix D2 methodology: generate entity-type absorption labels via IG pipeline on animal-specific SAE latents (not k-sparse probe proxy); pilot takes 15-30 min
Update proposal.md to reflect: (1) encoder_norm + O_jaccard as detection framework, (2) negative amortization gap as primary mechanistic finding, (3) cross-hierarchy methodology fix
Consider dropping H4 (phase transition) and H5 (safety attribution) to maintain paper scope and GPU budget
Generate proper Gemma Scope results (accept license at huggingface.co/google/gemma-scope) for cross-model validation

strategic_reframing_required.contribution_1: Replace ARS product (O_jaccard * A_cooccur * cos2) with encoder_norm + O_jaccard two-signal framework. A_cooccur adds no information due to mathematical ceiling. O_jaccard AUROC=0.721 is the highest-AUPRC result (0.075 vs. encoder_norm 0.004).
strategic_reframing_required.contribution_2: Reframe as decisive negative: 'OMP does not reduce absorption; encoder changes are insufficient; sparsity landscape is the root cause.' Both outcomes were pre-committed as publishable.
strategic_reframing_required.contribution_3: Two options: (a) Fix methodology and attempt to confirm/falsify H3; (b) Present null result as 'first-letter absorption does not trivially generalize to semantic hierarchies' and investigate the mechanism (why is entity-type AR=0%?). The unexpected 0% AR for entity-type with 100% probe accuracy is itself an interesting finding.
